calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Luke Federici, Esq., and Sonia Agee, Esq., counsel for and with Chi Ling Wong, Administrator (appearing separately by video conference).

The Court initially expresses concern regarding the manner of the administration of the estate and the process of the sale of the decedent's accounting practice. Ms. Agee responds, noting that because of the nature of the sale - an asset purchase agreement - and the nature of the practice - an accounting office - Ms. Wong proceeded in a manner that would best serve estate administration and protect the clients of the decedent.

After further discussion, the Court takes the matter under submission.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-20-2020 Continued To Aug-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Sonia Agee and Luke Federici appeared by telephone for Chi Ling Wong (Petitioner).

The Court questions why this petition should be dismissed when the Executor has a statutory duty to close the estate in a timely manner. Ms. Agee stated that Mr. Wan was an Accountant, that Mr. Wan died very suddenly, that there was quite a bit of confusion and that the Wife had to take action rather quickly and dealt with certain assets of the corporation. Ms. Agee also stated that the estate was opened in an abundance of caution, that issues of the corporation were addressed, and that the estate turned out to be very small. The Court noted that a petition for final distribution is mandated and that there is a minor child. Ms. Agee stated that the minor Son has an independent Guardian Ad Litem, that she believes Ethan (Son) will be turning 18 this year, that all of the parties are noticed, and that the main asset was the corporation. Ms. Agee also stated that there never was any separate property, and that all of the assets were either community property, or value of the corporation. In addition, Ms. Agee stated that assets are the financial accounts at about $66,000.00, personal property with minimal value and the professional corporation. The Court informed Counsel that a petition to terminate for lack of assets in the estate needs to be filed, and that the issue of community property needs to be shown to the Court's satisfaction, as well as the accounts and the pay on death accounts to eliminate the Court's concern that no separate property was in play. The Court also informed Counsel that the concerns are referenced in the Probate Examiner's notes. The Request for Dismissal or Second Amended First and Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to august 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-23-2019 Continued To Feb-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Luke T. Federici for Executor, Chi Ling Wong via Court Call.

The Court noted it did not see serious efforts to address the Probate Examiner's notes regarding community property. Mr. Federici stated that he was advised by the spouse the decedent's siblings and everyone agreed the bulk of the estate is from the business that acquired gain value during marriage, that he will supplement petition with evidence that everything decedent had was community property, and will file accounting.

The Amended First and Final Report on Waiver of Account, etc. is continued to February 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Supplement to be filed by January 21, 2020.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: C. Robles. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Oct-30-2018 Off Calendar

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Oct-16-2018 Continued To Oct-30-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jennifer Acheson, Esq. appearing by CourtCall for Gillian Wong (Petitioner).
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

The Court stated that the matter was on for status on the administration and for responses to the probate examiner notes. The Court further stated that courtesy copies along with a proposed order were requested. Ms Acheson stated that a petition for first and final account was being filed today, that they ran in to difficulties due in part to there being threats of litigation even though creditor claims were never filed, and that there were additional issues because the beneficiaries were in Hong Kong. The Court continued the matter to October 30, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 with the expectation that it may go off calendar if the new petition is on file.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy R. Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate.
Off Calendar Jun-04-2018 Continued To Jun-25-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jennifer Acheson for Gillian Wan.

Ms. Acheson stated that she e filed a petition for approval of status report, that she used One Legal, that she got notice that they do not do probate filings and that she is waiting for the appraisal from the Probate Referee. Status on close of estate is continued to June 25, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate.
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Continued To Jun-04-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jennifer Acheson for petitioner Chi Ling Wong.

Ms. Acheson apologized to the Court for her failure to appear at the last scheduled hearing date, and explained that she had been injured in a serious accident in which she suffered a concussion. She said that the closing of the estate had been complicated by the fact the decedent owned several businesses, including an accounting business which the executor had sold prior to engaging legal representation through Ms. Acheson's firm. She said that she had researched the estate and that she had received notice of one potential claim, but that the claims period had passed and that no creditor's claims had been filed. The Court directed her to either file a petition for first and final distribution, or a petition to approve status report within 90 days. The matter was continued to June 4, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.
The Order to Appear was taken off calendar.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Shirley Le. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-07-2018 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate.
Off Calendar Feb-21-2018 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Executor and the Attorney to appear on March 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution or petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
